title: UAV Mission Architecture
description: Develop a concept of operations and system architecture for a UAV mission such as infrastructure inspection, mapping, or emergency response.
whyItMatters: Many aerospace projects fail because the vehicle was designed before the operation was understood. A strong mission architecture prevents elegant hardware from solving the wrong problem.

steps.title: Define the mission scenario
steps.description: Choose the customer, operating environment, payload, endurance, and response-time expectations.
steps.tips: Mission architecture becomes sharper when one real scenario anchors it.

steps.title: Map the full operation
steps.description: Describe planning, launch, mission execution, communications, recovery, maintenance, and data handoff.
steps.tips: Include humans, software, regulators, and logistics in the story.

steps.title: Build the system architecture
steps.description: Break the solution into air vehicle, payload, autopilot, communications, ground station, and support infrastructure.
steps.tips: Interfaces matter more than boxes. Define what must pass between them.

steps.title: Identify the hard constraints
steps.description: Highlight the limiting factors such as radio coverage, battery reserve, observer requirements, or weather windows.
steps.tips: The real mission killer is often operational, not aerodynamic.

steps.title: Write the initial requirement set
steps.description: Turn the architecture into a shortlist of testable system requirements and open risks.
steps.tips: Use verbs like shall, must, and can only when the condition is measurable.

deliverables: Initial requirement set with top risks

rubric.description: The mission concept reflects actual people, constraints, and environmental conditions.
Subsystem boundaries and interfaces are clear and justified.
Requirements are testable and flow logically from the CONOPS.
The work surfaces the most important uncertainties early.
